Lily Lifesciences,Diksha Jaiswal,https://appinventivtech.atlassian.net/jira/software/c/projects/LL/boards/604,Yes,,,"1 - Story pointing is followed
2 - Multile sprint are created in the backlog, which is good, however see the active sprint I believe the past sprint are not marked as complete, due to which the Sprint 1 task are still active, If we are having any dependency on the task it is better to move the same to backlog and close the sprint.
3 - Version and Epic are managed in the project
4 - There are task for which sub tasks or linked issues are closed, however the user story is still in the inprogress state, it will be better if we can close such type of user stories
5 -  In few stories the status is marked as closed however the resolution is not declared due to which the user stories a

inprogress state, it will be better if we can close such type of user stories
5 -  In few stories the status is marked as closed however the resolution is not declared due to which the user stories are not fully marked as done, we can discuss on this pointer if you have any questions",
KODA,Tanvi Bhardwaj,https://trykoda.atlassian.net/jira/software/projects/KA/boards/1/backlog,"Yes(But right now we are not doing story pointing because it is in support and there are majorly bugs on which we are working on, and sometimes small tasks are there, Bugs we are reporting on Jira only, and following Jira to track the bugs status and share bug report sheet with client)",,,"- No Story pointing is followed
- Active sprint date is from May 14 - Jun 4. I suggest to have the date accurate so that the evaluation of work done can be done
- As we are working on bugs so I suggest to manage the workflow of the project with the Kanban approach.
- I can see one sprint named end to end testing on staging environment which have 25 isusues, most of them are marked as HOLD, I suggest to mode those to backlog and close the sprint.",
